title: 25. Piano Player: Auto-Range Keyboards
original_text: We kept the Pianola window moving in a useful direction: each track keyboard now narrows itself to the notes that track actually uses. What changed: In [widgets.py]..., `PianoKeyboard` now supports a configurable note range. In [sequence.py]..., `midi_track_infos()` now includes per-track `min_note` and `max_note`. In [app.py]..., the Pianola row keyboards pick up that range... I also updated: [Roadmap.md]... [EN guide]... [ES guide]... Tests were expanded in [test_app_playlist.py]... Verification is clean: ... 152 tests OK...
expected_subject: feat(ui): auto-range Pianola keyboards to used notes
expected_body_lines: - Add configurable note range support to PianoKeyboard widget
- Expose min/max note per track via midi_track_infos() in sequence.py
- Initialize each Pianola row keyboard with its specific note range
- Update user guides (EN/ES) and Roadmap.md with new behavior docs
- Add tests for per-track note range calculation and application
- Validation: compileall OK, 152 tests pass, offscreen verified
expected_command:
git commit -m "feat(ui): auto-range Pianola keyboards to used notes" \
  -m "- Add configurable note range support to PianoKeyboard widget" \
  -m "- Expose min/max note per track via midi_track_infos() in sequence.py" \
  -m "- Initialize each Pianola row keyboard with its specific note range" \
  -m "- Update user guides (EN/ES) and Roadmap.md with new behavior docs" \
  -m "- Add tests for per-track note range calculation and application" \
  -m "- Validation: compileall OK, 152 tests pass, offscreen verified"
